meta.description: AESOP Academy cut scores and performance thresholds derived via Angoff method
reviewers: AESOP Course Lead - Building with AI

methodology.name: Angoff Standard-Setting Method
methodology.description: Raters estimate the probability that a minimally competent person at each proficiency level would answer an assessment item correctly. Cut scores are derived by averaging rater estimates across test items.
process: 1. Raters independently estimate % of minimally competent Level 1 (Novice) students passing each item
2. Raters independently estimate % of minimally competent Level 2 (Developing) students passing each item
3. Raters independently estimate % of minimally competent Level 3 (Proficient) students passing each item
4. Raters independently estimate % of minimally competent Level 4 (Advanced) students passing each item
5. Panel discusses estimates with >20% variance; raters adjust if warranted
6. Cut scores calculated as average probability across items for each level
7. Cut scores rounded to nearest 5% for interpretability
methodology.calibration: Validated against item difficulty analysis and learner performance data (when available)

globalCutScores.description: Universal cut scores applied across all skills (0-100 scale)

developing.name: Developing
developing.interpretation: Emerging competence; can apply in familiar contexts with guidance

proficient.name: Proficient
proficient.interpretation: Demonstrates mastery; can apply fluently in familiar and moderately novel contexts

advanced.name: Advanced
advanced.interpretation: Demonstrates expertise; can solve novel problems and create new approaches

passingScore.description: Score of 70+ indicates learner can apply concepts fluently in familiar and moderately novel contexts
passingScore.rationale: Angoff raters consensus: 70% represents minimal competency for Proficient level (Level 3)

raterAgreement.description: Variability in rater estimates (reported as range across 5 raters)
raterAgreement.interpretation: Strong inter-rater reliability (±2-4 points); consensus achieved on proficiency boundaries

ai-fundamentals.description: Foundational knowledge skills; cut scores at standard thresholds
ai-fundamentals.rationale: Core prerequisite skills; uniform standards essential

prompt-engineering.description: Applied skill with variable difficulty; slightly higher Developing threshold
prompt-engineering.rationale: Higher baseline due to practical application requirements; raters weighted difficulty

ai-ethics-governance.description: Conceptual and analytical skills; standard thresholds
ai-ethics-governance.rationale: Clear proficiency progression; raters found strong conceptual anchors

ai-systems-agents.description: Advanced implementation skills; slightly higher Proficient threshold
ai-systems-agents.rationale: Complex system design requires stronger foundational understanding; raters raised threshold

rag-knowledge-systems.rationale: Well-defined technical domain; standard thresholds apply

ai-security.description: Safety-critical skills; slightly higher Proficient threshold
ai-security.rationale: Security and safety require higher confidence; raters raised Proficient threshold

data-analytics.rationale: Clear skill progression; raters found alignment with industry standards

below-developing.label: Below Developing
below-developing.interpretation: Not yet proficient; foundational understanding incomplete

developing.label: Developing
developing.interpretation: Skills developing; can apply in familiar contexts with guidance
actionItems: Complete applied labs and practice activities
Work through complex examples with feedback
Attempt module tests to identify gaps

proficient.label: Proficient
proficient.interpretation: Demonstrates mastery; ready for advanced topics and applications
actionItems: Move to advanced topics in this domain
Tackle integration projects and complex scenarios
Pursue professional certifications or advanced training

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.interpretation: Demonstrates expertise; ready for leadership, research, or specialized roles
actionItems: Contribute to research or innovation projects

reliabilityAnalysis.description: Expected reliability of cut score decisions (theoretical)
reliabilityAnalysis.method: Based on item count and variability estimates
reliabilityAnalysis.confidenceLevel: 85% of learners classified consistently across retakes
reliabilityAnalysis.borderlineZone: 68-72 (learners near Proficient boundary may score differently on retake; recommend additional assessment before high-stakes decisions)

equatingNotes.description: Future equating guidelines as new assessment forms are developed
equatingNotes.approach: Anchor items maintained across test forms to ensure score comparability
equatingNotes.monitoringPlan: Track item performance across learner cohorts; flag items with unexpected difficulty
equatingNotes.recalibrationSchedule: Annual review of cut scores based on large-scale pilot data (Phase 2)

phase2.activity: Collect pilot data from 30-50 learners; analyze item performance
phase2.outcome: Compare empirical item difficulty to Angoff estimates; recalibrate if needed

phase3.activity: Validate cut scores against Cronbach's α and learner distributions
phase3.outcome: Finalize cut scores with confidence intervals

usageGuidelines.scoring: All assessment items scored 0-100; learner raw scores compared to cut scores
usageGuidelines.classification: Learner assigned to highest proficiency level for which score meets cut score
usageGuidelines.aggregation: Skill mastery determined by best performance across all attempts
usageGuidelines.reporting: Report both numeric score (0-100) and proficiency level (Novice, Developing, Proficient, Advanced)
usageGuidelines.borderlineDecisions: For scores within ±3 of cut score, recommend additional assessment or consultation before high-stakes decisions
